records.ety: [F. diligent, L. diligens, -entis, p. pr. of diligere, dilectum, to esteem highly, prefer; di- = dis- + legere to choose. See Legend.]

defs: Prosecuted with careful attention and effort; careful; painstaking; not careless or negligent.
quotes: The judges shall make diligent inquisition.

defs: Interestedly and perseveringly attentive; steady and earnest in application to a subject or pursuit; assiduous; industrious.
quotes: Seest thou a man diligent in his business? he shall stand before kings.
